title: Samaṇa Gotama abstains from false speech, avoids false speech, speaks truth, adheres to truth, is reliable, trustworthy, does not deceive, does not break his word…
content: Samaṇa Gotama abstains from false speech, avoids false speech, speaks truth, adheres to truth, is reliable, trustworthy, does not deceive, does not break his word to the world. Samaṇa Gotama abstains from divisive speech, avoids divisive speech; what he hears here he does not repeat elsewhere to cause division among these people; what he hears elsewhere he does not repeat to these people to cause division among those people.

title: And, bhikkhus, how is a bhikkhu called an Arahant? He has made evil, unwholesome states that are defiled, leading to renewed existence, fearful, yielding painful…
content: And, bhikkhus, how is a bhikkhu called an Arahant? He has made evil, unwholesome states that are defiled, leading to renewed existence, fearful, yielding painful results, leading to future birth, aging, and death, far from him. Thus, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu is an Arahant.

title: Bhikkhus, as long as the bhikkhus delight in secluded dwellings, then the bhikkhus will prosper and not decline.
content: Bhikkhus, as long as the bhikkhus delight in secluded dwellings, then the bhikkhus will prosper and not decline.

title: Standing to one side, the devata Khemā spoke this verse before the Blessed One: Fools without wisdom Treat the self as an enemy, They do…
content:
Standing to one side, the devata Khemā spoke this verse before the Blessed One:

Fools without wisdom
Treat the self as an enemy,
They do evil deeds
That bring bitter fruit.
A deed not well done,
Once done, torments one,
With tearful face,
Weeping, one experiences its result.
A deed well done,
Once done, does not torment one,
With joyful mind, at ease,
That one experiences its result.
Knowing what is good for oneself,
One should do what should be done first,
Not with the mind of a charioteer,
The wise one's mind is energetic.
Like a charioteer
Leaving the even highway,
Ascending a rough road,
Grieves over a broken axle.
So too, abandoning the Dhamma,
The fool follows non-Dhamma,
Falls into the mouth of Death,
Grieves like a broken axle.

title: Having given up children and spouse, Father and mother, money and grain, Relatives and the bonds of sensual desire, Live alone like a rhinoceros. Knowing…
content:
Having given up children and spouse,
Father and mother, money and grain,
Relatives and the bonds of sensual desire,
Live alone like a rhinoceros.

Knowing that this is bondage, that the happiness is slight,
That the gratification is little, and the suffering far greater,
This is a hook; knowing this, the wise person,
Live alone like a rhinoceros.

title: Vāseṭṭha, just as this river Aciravati is full to the brim so that a crow could drink from it. A person comes, having business on…
content: Vāseṭṭha, just as this river Aciravati is full to the brim so that a crow could drink from it. A person comes, having business on the further shore, seeking the further shore, heading for the further shore, wishing to cross over to the further shore. This person, on this bank, covers his head and lies down to sleep. Vāseṭṭha, what do you think? Could that person cross from this bank of the river Aciravati to the further shore? — No, Master Gotama. — So too, Vāseṭṭha, there are five hindrances which are called obstacles, also called hindrances, also called coverings, also called fetters in the Discipline of the noble ones. What are the five? The hindrance of sensual desire, the hindrance of ill-will, the hindrance of sloth and torpor, the hindrance of restlessness and remorse, the hindrance of doubt. Vāseṭṭha, these five hindrances are called obstacles, hindrances, coverings, fetters. Vāseṭṭha, those brahmins proficient in the three Vedas are obstructed, hindered, covered, fettered by these five hindrances. Vāseṭṭha, that those brahmins proficient in the three Vedas, abandoning those qualities that make a brahmin, following those qualities that do not make a brahmin, being obstructed, hindered, covered, fettered by these five hindrances, after death, with the breakup of the body, should be reborn in companionship with Brahmā — this is impossible.

title: Byagghapajjā, this is called purity of virtue. With the resolution: 'If this purity of virtue is incomplete, I will complete it. If this purity of…
content: Byagghapajjā, this is called purity of virtue. With the resolution: 'If this purity of virtue is incomplete, I will complete it. If this purity of virtue is complete, I will learn further here and there with wisdom.' Here, the desire, energy, effort, exertion, non-backsliding, mindfulness, and clear comprehension — these, Byagghapajjā, are called the factor of striving for purity of virtue. Here, Byagghapajjā, a bhikkhu, secluded from sensual pleasures ... enters and abides in the fourth jhāna. This, Byagghapajjā, is called purity of mind. With the resolution: 'If this purity of mind is incomplete, I will complete it. If it is complete, I will learn further here and there with wisdom.' Here, the desire, energy, effort, exertion, non-backsliding, mindfulness, and clear comprehension — these, Byagghapajjā, are called the factor of striving for purity of mind.

title: Mother and father are called Brahmā, The ancient teachers, Worthy to be supported and honored by children. Therefore the wise pay homage and respect them,…
content:
Mother and father are called Brahmā,
The ancient teachers,
Worthy to be supported and honored by children.

Therefore the wise pay homage and respect them,
Offer food and drink,
Clothing and bedding,
Anoint their bodies,
Bathe their hands and feet.

By acting thus toward mother and father,
In this life the wise praise them,
In the next they enjoy heavenly bliss.

title: Elder Ajita: I have no fear of death, no desire for life. I will lay down this body, mindful and clearly comprehending.
content:
Elder Ajita:

I have no fear of death, no desire for life.
I will lay down this body, mindful and clearly comprehending.

title: Elder Isidatta: The five aggregates are fully known, standing with roots cut off. The end of suffering is attained — I have attained the destruction…
content:
Elder Isidatta:

The five aggregates are fully known, standing with roots cut off.
The end of suffering is attained — I have attained the destruction of taints.
